pi.name: Professor Brian Sia Jia Xu
pi.title: Principal Investigator
pi.affiliation: School of Electrical & Electronic Engineering, Nanyang Technological University
pi.biography:
Brian builds photonic systems that bridge the gap between laboratory breakthroughs and real world impact. A Singaporean electrical engineer, he specializes in high fidelity integrated photonic and ultra coherent laser platforms, the foundational technologies behind GPS free navigation, THz to MHz unification paradigms, radars, gyroscopes, free space communications, optoelectronic platform architecture, and large scale integration.

Brian received his Ph.D. from NTU, where he was elected Valedictorian of his graduating class. Along the way, he trained at the University of Strathclyde, UK, the Tokyo Institute of Technology, Japan, and Intel Labs, Santa Clara, US.

Before returning to academia, Brian served as Head of Optical and Product Engineering at a prominent Singapore based silicon photonics startup, where he architected and shipped commercial photonic platforms from the ground up. In 2022, he was one of only two annual recipients of the prestigious Ministry of Education NTU CoE International Postdoctoral Fellowship, which took him to the Massachusetts Institute of Technology from 2022 to 2024. He joined the School of Electrical and Electronic Engineering at NTU as an Assistant Professor in the fall of 2024.

Brian was the first researcher to establish a research collaboration with Samsung outside of South Korea and the US. He serves as a Principal Investigator at the DSO National Laboratories Quantum Sensing Centre and the National Center of Advanced Integrated Photonics (NCAIP). He holds 7 patents, 5 of which have been licensed to industry.

Brian has delivered keynotes and invited talks at venues including the Keysight Lightwave Symposium 2026, SEMICON Southeast Asia 2025 (Key National Capabilities), IEEE Photonics Conference 2025, and the inaugural Tech Talk at the DSO Quantum Sensing Centre. He chaired Photonics@SG 2025 (10th Anniversary), co-chaired Photonics@SG 2026, and has served as session chair at IEEE IPC 2025. He sits on the advisory board of Matter and Light, a Cell Press journal, and reviews for Advanced Optical Materials, Journal of Lightwave Technology, ACS Photonics, Optics Express/Letters, and JOSA B. Brian has been the corresponding author in journals such as Nature Communications, and his work has been covered by MIT News, Optics.org, and other international outlets.

Beyond the lab, Brian is building several stealth ventures at the intersection of photonics and deep tech, and serves as a principal consultant to multiple deep tech startups.
